result.markdown:
---
title: "04 ‚Äî Derived maps"
subtitle: "Compute a map that remembers how it was made."
jupyter: python3
sidebar: start
---

A lot of analysis collapses a 4-D BOLD across time into a 3-D map ‚Äî temporal SNR, a contrast, a searchlight statistic. The map you get back is a normal volume you can index, save, or plot; it just also remembers which image and mask produced it, so you can answer that question later without keeping notes.

## Temporal SNR ‚Äî the canonical case

::: {#a33feaef .cell execution_count=2}
``` {.python .cell-code}
from pathlib import Path
import sys

sys.path.insert(0, str(Path.cwd().parents[1] / "src"))

import neuroim as ni

repo = Path.cwd().parents[1] if Path.cwd().name == "start" else Path.cwd()
bold = ni.read_image(str(repo / "golden_tests/fixtures/tiny_bold.nii.gz"))
mask = ni.read_image(str(repo / "golden_tests/fixtures/tiny_mask.nii.gz"), type="vol")

tsnr = bold.temporal_snr(mask=mask)
type(tsnr).__name__, tsnr.shape
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1}
```
('DenseNeuroVol', (np.int64(8), np.int64(8), np.int64(4)))
```
:::
:::


`temporal_snr` returns a `DenseNeuroVol` ‚Äî the same type a 3-D NIfTI would read as. The numeric payload is in `tsnr.data`; the spatial frame is in `tsnr.space`. The new field is `.provenance`.

::: {#3871f342 .cell execution_count=3}
``` {.python .cell-code}
tsnr.provenance.method_name
tsnr.provenance.input_space_hash[:12]
tsnr.provenance.mask_hash[:12]
tsnr.provenance.n_voxels
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=2}
```
108
```
:::
:::


This is the same `Receipt` shape `series_roi` produced on the [previous page](03-roi.qmd), now living on a *volume* instead of on a `Result`. The split is deliberate:

- `ROIExtractionResult.provenance` belongs to an extraction ‚Äî a multi-voxel cross-section of a 4-D series.
- `DenseNeuroVol.provenance` belongs to a map ‚Äî a 3-D output of an analysis.

Both are `Receipt`s. Both content-address the same way.

## Searchlight ‚Äî the same shape, scaled

::: {#d6ced79c .cell execution_count=4}
``` {.python .cell-code}
import numpy as np

result = ni.searchlight_apply(mask, radius=4.5, method=np.mean, data=bold)
type(result).__name__, result.values.shape, result.provenance.method_name
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=3}
```
('SearchlightResult', (108,), 'mean')
```
:::
:::


`searchlight_apply` returns a `SearchlightResult` ‚Äî values, centers, space, Receipt. Project it back to volume form for downstream IO:

::: {#da33bb64 .cell execution_count=5}
``` {.python .cell-code}
vol = result.map_to_volume()
type(vol).__name__, vol.shape, vol.provenance.method_name
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=4}
```
('DenseNeuroVol', (np.int64(8), np.int64(8), np.int64(4)), 'mean')
```
:::
:::


The projected volume is again a `DenseNeuroVol` with `.provenance`. The shape is the same as `tsnr` above; the `method_name` distinguishes them. Two analyses, two maps, same carrier.

## Why the carrier matters

A workflow that produces N maps over N subjects ends up with a list of `DenseNeuroVol`s, each carrying its `.provenance`. A group-mean operation can verify every input's `input_space_hash` matches before reducing; the next step's Receipt records the inputs it consumed. None of this requires a sidecar manifest the user has to remember to write.

When you write `tsnr` to disk, `to_nibabel()` + `nib.save()` is the receipt-preserving write path. `ni.write_vol(...)` saves the array but not the Receipt, so reach for the explicit path whenever provenance needs to stay with the file ‚Äî the subject of the [next page](05-roundtrip.qmd).

## What it refuses

Skippable on a first read ‚Äî the edges, for when you hit them.

- **`temporal_snr(mask=...)` with a mask from another space.** The mask must share the BOLD image's spatial frame. Matching array shapes are not enough; the affine and dimensions are checked before the reduction runs.
- **`searchlight_apply(mask=..., data=bold)` with a shifted same-shape mask.** Shape agreement is not space agreement. `searchlight_apply` checks the mask's affine before building neighborhoods, so a shifted mask raises instead of mixing values from the wrong locations.
- **Hand back an anonymous 3-D array from a reduction.** Every reduction over a typed object returns a typed object. `temporal_snr` does not return `np.ndarray`; `searchlight_apply` does not return a tuple.

## Next

[05 ‚Äî Round-trip](05-roundtrip.qmd) is where the Receipt crosses the file boundary. The carrier on this page is `DenseNeuroVol` with `.provenance`; the carrier on the next page is `.nii.gz` with a `comment` extension. The contract is that the round-trip is provenance-safe.

## See also

- [Concept: Provenance](../concepts/provenance.qmd) ‚Äî what survives the file, what doesn't.
- [Concept: ROI result](../concepts/roi-result.qmd) ‚Äî the family `SearchlightResult` belongs to.
- [Evidence](../evidence/index.qmd) ‚Äî runnable checks for derived maps and searchlight workflows.
